canonical_meaning: Governance, assignment, provisioning, control, configuration, and ecosystem/admin input handling
primary_usage: Policy assignment; policy bundle intake; metadata directives; operational directives; safe-point replacement
used_by_entity_names: Assembler Subsystem;PolicyAssigner;Administrative / Ecosystem Port;Metadata Injector;Queue Bundle Agent;Assemblers Administrator;Policy Registry / Policy Source;Metadata Directive Source;Scaling / Operational Directive Source
governed_object_relevance: AssemblerPolicyBundle;Policy Object;metadata directive;operational directive;administrative surface;replacement-eligible Policy Object
anti_collapse_rules: administrative payloads must not enter through consuming ingress;PolicyAssigner must not be bypassed;Metadata Injector must not become policy host
query_examples: Find responsibilities entering through administrative path; Find all policy assignment responsibilities; Verify admin and consuming surfaces remain separated
notes: Administrative surface implements the upper layer of the Three Surface Law for governance and control input.

canonical_meaning: Runtime realization, orchestration, lifecycle handling, moderation, mechanism hosting, delivery control, and state progression
primary_usage: Queue runtime; dispatcher behavior; policy-governed mechanisms; envelope movement; lifecycle transfer; feedback state updates
used_by_entity_names: Assembler Subsystem;Dispatcher;Writing / Feeding Round Manager;Dispatching / Delivery Round Manager;Queue Bundle Agent;Single Queue Moderator;Metadata Injector;Timestamp Stabilizer;Validator;PolicyAssigner;Envelope Assembler;Registry / Delivery Port
governed_object_relevance: Log Level Envelope;Queue Bundle;Queue Container;Slot;Envelope Slot Occupancy;Slot Lifecycle State;Queue Container Identifier;Delivery-Cycle Flag Set;Dispatcher Feedback Report
anti_collapse_rules: mechanism hosting must remain inside components;Queue Runtime Layer must not become delegated entity;slot occupancy must not become envelope lifecycle ownership;flags must not become reports
query_examples: Find all runtime responsibilities; Find all queue moderation responsibilities; Find all Dispatcher managerial obligations
notes: Managerial surface is the execution-realization plane where assigned policies and runtime state are acted upon by concrete carriers.

canonical_meaning: Content-facing, API-facing, and consumer admission surface used for normal log content entry
primary_usage: Typed content ingress; validating/hot-path ingress split; envelope preparation through consumer-side contract object
used_by_entity_names: Level API;Envelope Assembler;Assembler Ingress Port;Assembler Subsystem
governed_object_relevance: typed content contract;ingress contract variants;hot-path contract variant;Log Level Envelope;ingress contract shape
anti_collapse_rules: consuming content ingress must not receive policy bundles;hot-path must not silently reintroduce local validation;raw content must not bypass envelope construction
query_examples: Find all responsibilities related to typed content entry; Verify hot-path ingress separation; Find where Log Level Envelope enters runtime
notes: Consuming surface is for user/API-originated content, not administrative control or registry delivery.

canonical_meaning: Authority edge, subsystem separation, handoff edge, lifecycle transfer boundary, and explicit exclusion surface
primary_usage: Dispatcher boundary; registry handoff; ingress admission; administrative boundary; read/query exclusion; queue-container terminology protection
used_by_entity_names: Assembler Subsystem;Dispatcher;Assembler Ingress Port;Administrative / Ecosystem Port;Registry / Delivery Port;Queue Bundle Agent;Single Queue Moderator;Log Level Records Registry;Reading / Querying Subsystem
governed_object_relevance: Log Level Envelope;readiness signal;receiving admission access;lifecycle transfer state;dispatcher boundary edge;Queue Container;Envelope Slot Occupancy;non-authority boundary
anti_collapse_rules: Dispatcher boundary must not be bypassed;Registry / Delivery Port must not replace Dispatcher;read/query must remain outside Assembler authority;Assembler must not own registry lifecycle after handoff;Queue must remain Container
query_examples: Find all boundary responsibilities; Verify registry boundary abstraction; Verify read/query exclusion; Find all responsibilities preserving Queue-as-Container boundary
notes: Boundary surface preserves authority separation and prevents false ownership or false actor promotion.

canonical_meaning: Typed/template contract law and admissibility surface replacing runtime COM-interface assumptions
primary_usage: Static/template contract enforcement; contract-shape verification; port contract surfaces; validating/hot-path split; outbound registry contract law
used_by_entity_names: Envelope Assembler;Assembler Ingress Port;Administrative / Ecosystem Port;Registry / Delivery Port;Timestamp Stabilizer;Validator;Level API
governed_object_relevance: typed content contract;ingress contract variants;AssemblerPolicyBundle;metadata directive;operational directive;receiving admission access;Log Level Envelope
anti_collapse_rules: contracts must be typed/template laws not runtime COM interfaces;contract families must not collapse;validating and hot-path ingress variants must remain distinct
query_examples: Find all responsibilities depending on typed/template doctrine; Find all port contract surfaces; Verify no runtime COM interface dependency
notes: Contractual surface encodes the template/static-polymorphism doctrine governing ingress, admin, and registry-facing contracts.

canonical_meaning: Reporting, diagnostics, visibility, feedback artifacts, and diagnostic consumption surface
primary_usage: Detailed dispatcher feedback reports; diagnostic exposure; backpressure/failure/refusal reporting
anti_collapse_rules: detailed feedback reports must not be collapsed into delivery-cycle flags;reports are diagnostic artifacts not runtime state toggles
query_examples: Find all dispatcher reporting responsibilities; Verify detailed reports are separate from delivery flags
notes: Observability surface is narrower than EN-FAM-006 because EN-FAM-006 also includes delivery-cycle flag state updates.

canonical_meaning: Composite classification used where a responsibility explicitly preserves separation across multiple surfaces
primary_usage: Three Surface Law; boundary separation; doctrine preservation across consuming/admin/managerial planes
used_by_entity_names: Assembler Subsystem;Assembler Ingress Port;Administrative / Ecosystem Port;Registry / Delivery Port;Envelope Assembler;PolicyAssigner
governed_object_relevance: architectural surfaces;typed contract templates;Policy Object mechanism semantics
anti_collapse_rules: consuming/admin/managerial surfaces must not collapse;Policy Object governance must not collapse into component execution;contract templates must not collapse into runtime COM interface
query_examples: Find responsibilities enforcing Three Surface Law; Find cross-surface doctrine responsibilities
notes: Cross-surface is included for queryability where a responsibility is explicitly about preserving separation between surfaces.
